source_policy: primary organizational pages, official documentation, or primary research papers

sources.title: Why SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities
sources.observed: OpenAI found issues in at least 59.4 percent of a 138-task hard subset and reported evidence consistent with contamination.
sources.telos_implication: A benchmark score is not interpretable without task-validity and contamination audits.

sources.title: Separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
sources.observed: OpenAI estimates roughly 30 percent of SWE-Bench Pro public tasks are broken after agent-assisted and engineer review.
sources.telos_implication: Benchmark validity and low-coverage tests are first-order research targets.

sources.title: How we monitor internal coding agents for misalignment
sources.observed: The monitor examines full conversations, reasoning, actions, tool calls, and outputs.
sources.telos_implication: Future TELOS evidence must include full trajectories.

sources.title: Agent evals
sources.observed: OpenAI documents reproducible agent evaluation through datasets, trace grading, and continuous evaluation.
sources.telos_implication: TELOS should expose a reusable trace-evaluation API rather than only iteration-specific scripts.

sources.title: Agentic Misalignment in Summer 2026
sources.observed: Simulated cases include covert pipeline sabotage and consequence-sensitive judge mislabeling.
sources.telos_implication: LLM judges cannot define independent semantic ground truth.

sources.title: Pre-deployment auditing of an overt saboteur
sources.observed: Anthropic studies whether a deliberately misaligned model can be discovered through a structured pre-deployment audit.
sources.telos_implication: Assurance should be adversarial, staged before deployment, and explicit about audit power and blind spots.

sources.title: The NASA Software Engineering and Software Assurance Electronic Handbook
sources.observed: NASA emphasizes independent assurance, source-of-truth anchoring, configuration control, and human approval.
sources.telos_implication: TELOS independence must be organizational and procedural, not merely another model role.

sources.title: Reducing risk in software-driven hazards across NASA programs
sources.observed: NASA describes software assurance and independent verification and validation as lifecycle controls for software-driven hazards.
sources.telos_implication: Agent evidence gates should connect software behavior to hazard and consequence analysis across the lifecycle.

sources.title: AI and Robotics
sources.observed: Tesla describes open-loop, closed-loop, and hardware-in-the-loop evaluation infrastructure at fleet scale.
sources.telos_implication: Consequence replay and regression suites should cover the operational envelope.

sources.title: Vehicle Safety Report methodology
sources.observed: Tesla publishes an operational safety-report methodology based on fleet exposure and observed outcomes.
sources.telos_implication: Laboratory verifier metrics and deployment outcomes must remain separate evidence planes.

sources.title: Falcon User's Guide
sources.observed: SpaceX describes regular hardware-in-the-loop verification over a complete mission profile.
sources.telos_implication: A final-output check is not a full-system verification.

sources.title: Devin Best Practices
sources.observed: Cognition recommends small isolated verifiable slices and human review before merge.
sources.telos_implication: Deployment gates should constrain scope and compound-error risk.

sources.title: DRACO Benchmark
sources.observed: DRACO uses 100 production-grounded tasks, expert rubrics averaging roughly 40 criteria, peer review, and three judges.
sources.telos_implication: Real-use task sampling and expert criteria are stronger than a single gold differential.

sources.title: Frontiers in AI Evaluation
sources.observed: Stanford emphasizes causal, process-level, and real-world outcome evaluation.
sources.telos_implication: TELOS should measure goals, plans, actions, consequences, and deployment outcomes.

sources.title: Agent Island
sources.observed: Agent Island uses multi-agent games and adaptive opponents to resist benchmark saturation and contamination.
sources.telos_implication: Fresh adaptive tasks are a stronger long-run evaluation surface than repeatedly exposed static tasks.

sources.title: Towards a Science of AI Agent Reliability
sources.observed: Reliability is separated into consistency, robustness, predictability, and safety rather than reduced to accuracy.
sources.telos_implication: Repeated runs, perturbations, calibration, and severity belong in the next experiment.

sources.title: AI Agent Index safety analysis
sources.observed: Only four of 30 surveyed agents published agent-specific system cards, and third-party testing was usually absent.
sources.telos_implication: Transparent agent-specific evidence and independent testing can be a TELOS differentiator.

sources.title: SpecBench
sources.observed: Thirty systems-level tasks compare visible feature tests with held-out compositional tests.
sources.telos_implication: TELOS must not claim scale or priority and should focus on assurance and real-task custody.

sources.title: RewardHackingAgents
sources.observed: Workspace edits and file access are logged while reported metrics are checked against trusted reference metrics.
sources.telos_implication: Evaluator integrity and data leakage require separate controls.

sources.title: Hack-Verifiable Environments
sources.observed: Reward-hacking opportunities are embedded so exploitation can be labeled deterministically.
sources.telos_implication: Deterministic hack-verifiable opportunities are stronger calibration controls than post-hoc judge labels.

sources.title: SLSA provenance v1.2
sources.observed: SLSA defines provenance predicates that bind build outputs to identified inputs and build processes.
sources.telos_implication: Receipt v2 should evolve toward interoperable provenance rather than a TELOS-only envelope.

sources.title: in-toto Statement v1
sources.observed: The in-toto statement supplies a common subject-and-predicate envelope for verifiable attestations.
sources.telos_implication: TELOS claims should be serializable as standard attestations with explicit subjects.

sources.title: Sigstore keyless signing overview
sources.observed: Sigstore documents identity-bound keyless signing and transparency-log integration for artifacts.
sources.telos_implication: Public receipts need signer identity and external chronology in addition to hashes.
